description: PostgREST is an open-source server that turns a PostgreSQL database schema into a RESTful API. It uses database roles and permissions to control what API clients can access.
summary: PostgREST exposes a self-hosted HTTP REST API per deployment, with a generated OpenAPI document at each server’s root path, plus a local `postgrest` CLI binary; no GraphQL or MCP surfaces were found.

surfaces.name: PostgREST HTTP API
surfaces.spec: root path OpenAPI served by each PostgREST server instance is documented at https://docs.postgrest.org/en/v12/references/api/openapi.html
